architecture.name: SM_70 Programmer-Visible Logical Hardware
architecture.description: Logical GPU hardware model exposed to CUDA programmers. Physical implementation details that cannot be directly controlled are intentionally hidden.
notes: This view keeps execution, memory, and synchronization concepts that programmers can reason about or influence.
Internal cluster topology, warp scheduler micro-policy, cache fabric wiring, and exact execution-pipeline counts are hidden implementation details.

components.name: Host CPU
components.role: Launches kernels, manages streams, performs memory allocation, and initiates transfers.

components.name: GPU Device
components.role: Executes kernels and owns device-visible memory spaces.

components.name: Host-Device Transfer Path
components.role: Logical path for cudaMemcpy, unified memory migration, and peer-visible transfers. The exact physical bus or bridge is hidden.

kernel.role: A function launched on the GPU.

grid.role: A kernel launch instance composed of many thread blocks.

thread_block.role: A programmer-defined group of threads that can synchronize and share per-block shared memory.
programmer_controls: cooperative work partitioning within the block

warp.role: The main SIMT execution group visible to programmers for divergence, ballots, shuffles, and warp-level synchronization.

thread.role: The scalar programming context with its own registers, local state, and thread indices.

sm.role: The logical compute target that runs resident thread blocks and their warps.
programmer_can_influence: parallelism via grid and block sizing

standard_compute_pipelines.role: General arithmetic, logical, address, and load/store execution resources used by normal CUDA instructions.
standard_compute_pipelines.visibility: Programmers use them through instructions but do not directly control pipeline routing.

tensor_core_operations.role: Matrix-acceleration capability exposed through WMMA or MMA instructions.
tensor_core_operations.visibility: Programmers can target this capability explicitly, but exact hardware scheduling remains hidden.

registers.role: Fastest logical storage for thread-private values.
programmer_can_influence: occupancy trade-offs caused by register use

shared_memory.scope: per thread block on an SM
shared_memory.role: Programmer-managed on-chip memory for communication and data reuse within a block.

local_memory.role: Holds thread-private data that does not fit in registers or has address-taken semantics.

global_memory.role: Main device memory visible to all threads and the host through API operations.

constant_memory.role: Read-only memory space optimized for broadcast-like access patterns.

texture_surface_memory.role: Read or read-write access paths exposed through texture or surface APIs. Exact cache hierarchy is hidden.

host_memory.role: CPU-visible memory that can participate in host-device transfers, mapping, or unified-memory workflows.

block_level.name: Block Synchronization
block_level.role: Threads in a block can synchronize and communicate through shared memory.

warp_level.name: Warp Synchronization
warp_level.role: Threads in a warp can coordinate through warp intrinsics and warp-scope synchronization.

device_memory_ordering.name: Fences and Atomics
device_memory_ordering.role: Programmers can control visibility and ordering through atomics and memory fence instructions.

streams_and_events.name: Asynchronous Execution Control
streams_and_events.role: Programmers can orchestrate overlap, ordering, and dependency between kernels and copies using streams and events.

hidden_implementation_details: exact number and topology of warp schedulers
exact counts of arithmetic pipelines unless exposed by an API contract
summary_hierarchy: Host launches kernels and manages transfers.
Kernel -> Grid -> Thread Block -> Warp -> Thread is the main execution hierarchy visible to programmers.
SM is the logical execution target for resident blocks, but its internal scheduling policy is hidden.
Registers, shared, local, global, constant, texture/surface, and host memory are the main programmer-visible memory spaces.
Synchronization is exposed at warp, block, and memory-ordering levels through CUDA primitives.
